nodes.name: Weekly schedule
nodes.notes: Enable after the Google Sheet, Gmail credentials, and search queries are configured.

assignments.name: gmailOrderQuery
assignments.value: newer_than:14d (receipt OR order OR invoice OR shipped OR delivered) -category:promotions

assignments.name: gmailDealQuery
assignments.value: newer_than:7d (sale OR flyer OR coupon OR offer OR promo OR deals)

nodes.name: Configuration
nodes.notes: Central placeholders. Do not hard-code secrets in this workflow export.

nodes.name: Search receipt/order emails
nodes.notes: Tune the Gmail query before enabling the schedule. This should collect candidate evidence only.

nodes.name: Search deal/promo emails
nodes.notes: Deals are appended to Deals_Raw, not Purchases.

nodes.name: Prepare Orders_Raw rows
nodes.notes: Conservative transform: one email becomes one raw candidate row. Later parsing can split item lines.

nodes.name: Prepare Deals_Raw rows
nodes.notes: Deals are evidence for recommendations, not purchases.

nodes.name: Append Orders_Raw
nodes.notes: Append only. Do not overwrite raw evidence rows.

nodes.name: Append Deals_Raw
nodes.notes: Append only. Review before using deal rows in recommendations.

parameters.content:
## Review boundary

This workflow intentionally appends to `Orders_Raw` and `Deals_Raw` only. It does not write to `Purchases`, `Stock`, or `Budget_Export`.

Promotion into authoritative state should happen through review/promotion logic, not direct email automation.
